skill.name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter-java
skill.description: Guides Java developers through Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry exporter setup, span creation, metrics, exception recording, and migration to autoconfigure.
skill.summary: Use Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry patterns for Java observability and exporter migration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command, network, sensitive, and weak crypto patterns, but review found documentation examples rather than executable skill code. The command findings are Markdown code fences and inline package names, while network references are expected Azure Monitor, Maven, GitHub, OpenTelemetry, and Microsoft documentation links. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, hidden scripts, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are Markdown XML, Bash, and Java examples. They document Maven dependencies, environment variables, OpenTelemetry setup, spans, metrics, and migration steps, but SKILL.md contains no executable script body or command runner. Verdict: false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are visible fenced documentation blocks, not Ruby backticks or runtime command execution. No code path in the skill executes these examples automatically.

low_findings.title: Expected Azure Monitor Network References
low_findings.description: The network findings are expected for an observability skill that sends telemetry to Azure Monitor and links to official Azure, Maven, OpenTelemetry, GitHub, and Microsoft documentation. No unauthorized endpoint, hidden callback, or data exfiltration instruction was found. Verdict: false positive with normal user disclosure needs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains and endpoint pattern align with Azure Monitor documentation and the skill's stated purpose. The skill does not instruct the assistant to transmit local secrets or repository data.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Connection String Requires Secret Handling Care
low_findings.description: The skill shows an Application Insights connection string environment variable with placeholder values. This is not a real exposed secret, but users should avoid committing real connection strings and should store them through approved secret management. Verdict: low risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The value uses placeholder text rather than a live credential. The only remaining concern is that the documentation discusses a secret-bearing configuration field.

low_findings.title: Deprecated Package Is Clearly Disclosed
low_findings.description: The package is marked deprecated and the skill recommends migration to azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure. This is not malicious, but users should prefer the replacement package for new work. Verdict: low operational risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The deprecation is explicitly stated in the skill and migration guidance is provided. The risk is stale implementation guidance, not hostile behavior.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Directory and Weak Crypto Findings Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: Static sensitive and weak cryptographic algorithm findings appear to come from Azure telemetry terminology, deprecation wording, and Application Insights references. No Azure credential directory path, real credential material, cryptographic implementation, or weak algorithm usage was found. Verdict: false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic code or credential directory references at the cited locations. The matches are semantic false positives from documentation text.

content.value_statement: Java teams need reliable telemetry without hand-building every OpenTelemetry setup step. This skill provides focused Azure Monitor exporter examples, migration guidance, and observability patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry exporter setup for Java applications.
Shows how to configure telemetry with an Application Insights connection string.
Demonstrates spans, span attributes, nested spans, and exception recording.
Covers OpenTelemetry metrics with counters and histograms.
Links to Azure, Maven, OpenTelemetry, and Application Insights references.
limitations: The documented exporter package is deprecated for new projects.
The skill is a documentation guide and does not validate live Azure configuration.
Examples use placeholder values and require project-specific dependency versions.
It does not cover every OpenTelemetry semantic convention or Azure Monitor feature.

use_cases.title: Add Java Tracing
use_cases.description: Add basic span creation, attributes, exception recording, and scope management to a Java service.

use_cases.title: Plan Exporter Migration
use_cases.description: Replace the deprecated exporter dependency with Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry autoconfigure guidance.

use_cases.title: Standardize Observability Examples
use_cases.description: Create team guidance for Java metrics, traces, span naming, and Azure Monitor references.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for a Java service using this skill. Keep it practical and mention deprecation.

prompt_templates.title: Add Span Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add tracing to a Java operation with span names, attributes, exception recording, and correct span ending.

prompt_templates.title: Review Migration Steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my plan to migrate from azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter to azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure. Identify missing steps and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design Observability Standards
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an observability standard for Java services using Azure Monitor, OpenTelemetry spans, metrics, exception handling, and secret handling rules.

output_examples.input: Explain what this skill helps with.
output_examples.output: A concise guide that explains Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry setup for Java, including tracing, metrics, exceptions, and migration to autoconfigure.

output_examples.input: Help me migrate away from the deprecated package.
output: Testing steps for traces and metrics.

output_examples.input: Create best practices for Java telemetry.
output_examples.output: A short standards document covering span names, attributes, exception recording, metrics, semantic conventions, and secure configuration handling.

best_practices: Use azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure for new Java projects.
Store Application Insights connection strings in approved secret management.
Use clear span names, relevant attributes, and consistent exception recording.
anti_patterns: Do not commit real connection strings or instrumentation keys to source control.
Do not start new projects on the deprecated exporter package.
Do not add high-cardinality or sensitive values as span attributes.

faq.question: Is this skill for the current Azure Monitor Java package?
faq.answer: It documents the deprecated exporter and recommends migration to azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure.

faq.question: Does the skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It contains documentation examples and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it help with Application Insights connection strings?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows where connection strings are used, but users must provide and protect their own values.

faq.question: Does it cover traces and metrics?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for spans, attributes, nested spans, exceptions, counters, and histograms.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The main concern is proper handling of telemetry connection strings and avoiding sensitive data in telemetry attributes.
